elements.description: Primary nav link to the cart page, includes live item count in the label
elements.aria_notes: Link text is 'Cart (N)' where N is the cart count. Nested <span data-testid='cart-count'> holds the number.

elements.description: Numeric badge inside the cart nav link reflecting total items in cart
elements.aria_notes: Purely numeric text content. No role. Best reached via test id because the number itself isn't a stable accessible name.

elements.description: Checkbox filter on the home page that shows only products in the Planters category
elements.aria_notes: Wrapped in its own <label>; Planters is the only checkbox with that exact text.

elements.description: Range input controlling the maximum visible product price on the home page
elements.aria_notes: Native range input. Playwright's fill() does not work on ranges — use locator.evaluate or keyboard arrows to adjust, or dispatch an input event.

elements.description: Add to Cart button on the Terracotta Planter product card
elements.ideal_locator: getByRole('listitem').filter({ hasText: 'Terracotta Planter' }).getByRole('button', { name: 'Add to Cart' })
elements.aria_notes: Six product cards each have an 'Add to Cart' button — scoping by product is required. The button's class list includes a randomized 'btn-cta-XXXX' suffix that changes on every page load.

elements.description: Link styled as a button that navigates from the cart to the checkout page
elements.aria_notes: An anchor element with role='button'. Playwright's getByRole('link') matches its native role; getByRole('button') matches the ARIA role. Both are acceptable.

elements.description: Per-row Remove button in the cart items table
elements.aria_notes: Every row has its own Remove button. Visible text is identical on all of them ('Remove'), but each carries a product-specific aria-label ('Remove <product name>'). Relying on visible text alone will match multiple elements.

elements.description: Running subtotal below the cart items table
elements.aria_notes: Text content is a currency-formatted string. Use toHaveText('$X.XX') or a regex assertion; do not read via textContent without awaiting.

elements.description: Full name text input on the checkout form
elements.aria_notes: Standard label-wrapped input. getByLabel is the canonical choice.

elements.description: ZIP code input on the checkout form — intentionally missing a <label>
elements.aria_notes: INTENTIONAL TRAP: this input has no associated <label>, so getByLabel('ZIP code') will not find it. The placeholder is the only accessible name source — getByPlaceholder or getByRole('textbox', { name }) (which reads the placeholder as an accessible name) work. Tests should recognize this and choose accordingly.

elements.description: Final purchase submission button on the checkout form
elements.aria_notes: INTENTIONAL TRAP: on click, button text transitions 'Complete Purchase' -> 'Processing...' for ~1.5s, then the form is hidden and the order confirmation appears. Tests must either (a) wait for the confirmation to be visible, or (b) assert the intermediate 'Processing...' state, but must not assert on 'Complete Purchase' after clicking. Class list also includes a randomized 'btn-cta-XXXX' suffix.

elements.description: Order confirmation panel shown after a successful checkout submission
elements.aria_notes: Section is initially hidden; revealed 1.5s after submit. aria-live='polite' announces to assistive tech. Use toBeVisible() — it auto-retries until the element is attached and displayed.

elements.description: Dynamic order number rendered inside the confirmation panel
elements.aria_notes: Content is a six-digit number generated at submit time. Do not assert on an exact value — match a pattern like /^\d{6}$/.

elements.description: The product grid list that shows or hides cards based on active category filters
elements.aria_notes: The <ul id='product-grid'> is the container. When a filter is active, non-matching <li> cards get a CSS class that hides them; the list itself is always present.

elements.description: Individual product card list item within the product grid
elements.aria_notes: Each <li class='product-card'> has a data-category attribute ('planters', 'tools', 'seeds'). When filtered, non-matching cards receive class 'hidden' and display:none. toBeHidden() and toBeVisible() are the right assertions.
intentional_traps: hidden_class_trap: cards are hidden via CSS class, not removed from DOM — toBeHidden() correctly detects this, but checking count() will still return 6
